title: China-to-India Household Refrigerator Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: BEE Star Labelling — Frost-Free and Direct-Cool Refrigerators
rows.target_requirement: India's Bureau of Energy Efficiency operates the Standards and Labelling programme under the Energy Conservation Act framework. Frost-free refrigerators and direct-cool refrigerators are listed in the mandatory appliance scheme. Manufacturers, importers, or sellers must obtain BEE permission/model registration, use the approved star label, display the label online and offline, and pay applicable labelling fees. The BEE label is separate from BIS safety/market-access approval.
target_standards: Energy Conservation Act, 2001 — legal basis for BEE Standards and Labelling
Bureau of Energy Efficiency (Appliance Labelling and Compliance) Regulations, 2026 — registration, label display, fees, and compliance controls
BEE Mandatory Scheme listing — Frost Free Refrigerators
BEE Mandatory Scheme listing — Direct Cool Refrigerators
rows.china_equivalent: China uses the China Energy Label system and refrigerator energy grading under GB 12021.2. The label format, grade scale, registration workflow, test method, validity cycle, and surveillance rules differ from India's BEE star labelling programme. A Chinese Grade 1 rating does not automatically convert to a BEE star rating.
china_standards: China Energy Label framework — domestic energy label system
GB 12021.2 — Chinese refrigerator energy-efficiency grade test basis
rows.gap: Exporters must re-rate the model under BEE's refrigerator category and current label period. Required work typically includes India-specific model registration, test report mapping, star-rating calculation, label artwork approval, QR/code or label data controls, packaging and e-commerce label display, and fee reporting. Chinese CEL artwork and grade claims should be removed or separated from India-facing consumer claims unless specifically permitted.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory under the Energy Conservation Act / BEE regulations for frost-free and direct-cool refrigerators listed in the BEE mandatory scheme. BEE technical schedules are conformity benchmarks and rating methods, not standalone legal mandates.
mandatory_status.cn: China Energy Label is a China domestic programme and does not substitute for BEE permission or India star label display.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] BEE star labelling is a separate hard gate for frost-free and direct-cool refrigerators in India. Chinese energy grades do not substitute for BEE model registration, approved India label artwork, point-of-sale display, or BEE compliance reporting.

rows.requirement: BIS Market Access — Household Refrigerators (IS 1476 / IS 7872)
rows.target_requirement: Household refrigerators imported into India must be screened against the BIS Act, relevant Quality Control Orders, and the applicable BIS conformity assessment route before commercial sale. For refrigerator product families, Indian Standards such as IS 1476 and IS 7872 are used as technical conformity benchmarks for performance, construction, terminology, rating, marking, or test-method alignment depending on the model type. The legal obligation comes from Indian law, orders, and BIS licence/registration conditions; the Indian Standards themselves are conformity benchmarks and do not automatically become interchangeable with Chinese CCC/GB test evidence.
target_standards: Bureau of Indian Standards Act, 2016 — legal basis for BIS conformity assessment and standard mark controls
BIS Conformity Assessment Regulations, 2018 — certification, registration, and licence framework
IS 1476 — refrigerator-related Indian Standard used as a conformity benchmark where applicable
IS 7872 — refrigerator-related Indian Standard used as a conformity benchmark where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China's domestic pathway for household refrigerators normally relies on CCC for regulated safety/EMC aspects, China Energy Label for energy efficiency, and GB/GB-T standards such as GB 4706.13 and GB 12021.2 as technical bases. These approvals are domestic China compliance evidence. India does not treat CCC certificates or China Energy Label registrations as BIS approvals, and an Indian importer cannot clear or sell products solely on the basis of Chinese certificates.
china_standards: China CCC framework — domestic certification route for regulated appliance safety and EMC
GB 4706.13 — Chinese refrigerator safety test basis used under CCC where applicable
GB 12021.2 — Chinese refrigerator energy-efficiency grade test basis
rows.gap: The practical gap is legal identity and product-family mapping. The Indian importer/manufacturer must confirm whether each refrigerator type falls under an active BIS order, identify the correct conformity route, map model numbers and critical components to the BIS application, and submit India-acceptable test evidence. Existing China CCC files may be useful engineering inputs but do not replace BIS application data, Indian Standard mapping, local marking controls, or post-market surveillance obligations.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where covered by Indian law, BIS orders, licence conditions, or registration conditions. IS 1476 and IS 7872 are used as conformity benchmarks / presumption tools where applicable; do not treat the standards themselves as the legal mandate.
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic certificates are not a substitute for India BIS market access. Chinese GB/GB-T standards are domestic technical bases and do not create India compliance.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat BIS market access as a separate India legal gate. Map the refrigerator family to the current BIS order and Indian Standard references, then prepare India-specific test and registration/licence evidence. CCC and China Energy Label evidence can support engineering review but cannot replace BIS approval.

rows.requirement: Import via BIS/BEE Registration — Indian Importer, Labels, and Clearance File
rows.target_requirement: Refrigerators shipped from China to India need an India-facing clearance file that aligns the commercial invoice, model identifier, importer of record, BIS licence/registration status, BEE model registration and star label data, safety test evidence, refrigerant declarations, product labels, and packaging markings. BIS and BEE approvals are not merely website listings; the importer must ensure the exact imported model, brand, factory, and label data match the approved record.
target_standards: BIS Act, 2016 — conformity assessment and standard mark controls
Energy Conservation Act, 2001 — BEE labelling controls
BEE Appliance Labelling and Compliance Regulations, 2026 — registration, model permission, display, and compliance obligations
Indian customs/import documentation controls — importer of record and product identity matching
rows.china_equivalent: China export files often contain CCC certificates, China Energy Label data, factory inspection records, Chinese labels, and Chinese model families. India requires a separate importer-facing file. If the Chinese export model uses a private-label brand, alternate model number, or India-only rated configuration, those differences must be covered in the BIS/BEE records rather than assumed from China domestic approvals.
china_standards: China CCC framework — domestic market certificate and factory surveillance system
China Energy Label framework — domestic model and label data system
rows.gap: Before shipment, reconcile the bill of materials, model hierarchy, label artwork, ratings, importer name/address, country-of-origin statements, refrigerant charge, packaging, and online listing data against the India approvals. Common blockers include model suffix mismatch, BEE star label not approved for the imported configuration, missing Indian importer details, outdated refrigerant declaration, and factory/brand mismatch with BIS registration.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where Indian import, BIS, BEE, customs, or product-specific legal controls apply. Indian Standards in the file are conformity benchmarks / presumption tools; shipment legality depends on the applicable Act, order, licence, registration, and customs requirements.
mandatory_status.cn: China export clearance and domestic approvals do not substitute for India importer-of-record, BIS/BEE record matching, or India label controls.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Build a shipment-level India compliance pack before export: BIS record, BEE record, importer details, label artwork, refrigerant declaration, model mapping, and invoice/packing-list identity should all match. Do not rely on China domestic approvals at customs or point of sale.

rows.requirement: Refrigerant Controls — Ozone Depleting Substances Rules and Low-GWP Transition
rows.target_requirement: India regulates ozone-depleting substances under the Environment (Protection) Act framework and the Ozone Depleting Substances (Regulation and Control) Rules and amendments. The Ozone Cell notice states that HCFC-141b import licensing is prohibited from 1 January 2020 and that use of HCFCs in manufacturing all new equipment is prohibited from 1 January 2025. Refrigerator exporters must document refrigerant and foam-blowing agents, confirm no prohibited ODS/HCFC use, and verify flammable-refrigerant safety where R600a or other hydrocarbons are used.
target_standards: Environment (Protection) Act, 1986 — legal basis for ODS rules
Ozone Depleting Substances (Regulation and Control) Rules and amendments — refrigerant and foam blowing-agent controls
Montreal Protocol / Kigali Amendment implementation in India — phaseout and phasedown framework
rows.china_equivalent: China also implements Montreal Protocol and Kigali Amendment controls through its own national schedule and regulator. China export models commonly use R600a and cyclopentane for domestic refrigerators, but legacy refrigerants or foam agents may remain in older platforms or service-part documentation. India's prohibition dates and import licensing controls must be checked independently.
china_standards: China Montreal Protocol / Kigali Amendment implementation framework
GB 4706.13 — Chinese appliance safety test basis for flammable refrigerant aspects where applicable
rows.gap: The India file should include a refrigerant bill of materials, compressor nameplate data, charge quantity, SDS, foam-blowing-agent declaration, service instructions, warning labels for A3 refrigerants, and importer declarations for customs or regulator requests. R600a is generally compatible with low-GWP policy direction, but flammability controls and charge documentation must be reviewed under India safety expectations.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory under the Environment (Protection) Act / ODS Rules for controlled substances and licensing prohibitions. Safety standards for flammable refrigerants are conformity benchmarks / presumption tools.
mandatory_status.cn: China refrigerant compliance does not automatically satisfy India's ODS phaseout dates, import licensing position, or importer declarations.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not ship India models until refrigerant and foam-agent declarations have been checked against India's ODS rules and current Ozone Cell notices. R600a models still need charge, warning, and service documentation suitable for Indian safety and import review.

source.publisher: Ozone Cell, Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, Government of India

rows.requirement: Electrical Safety — IS 302 Series for Household Appliances
rows.target_requirement: India uses the IS 302 household-appliance safety series as the core technical benchmark for electrical safety of household and similar appliances, including refrigerator-relevant general safety requirements and particular requirements where applicable. The legal obligation arises from Indian law, BIS orders, licence/registration conditions, or importer obligations; IS 302 is the technical conformity benchmark used to demonstrate safety. Key areas include protection against electric shock, insulation, leakage current, earthing, temperature rise, abnormal operation, mechanical strength, supply cord, marking, and instructions.
target_standards: Bureau of Indian Standards Act, 2016 — legal basis for BIS conformity assessment
BIS Conformity Assessment Regulations, 2018 — licence, registration, and surveillance framework
IS 302 series — household and similar electrical appliance safety conformity benchmark
rows.china_equivalent: China's refrigerator safety evidence is commonly built around CCC and GB 4706.13 with GB 4706.1. The Chinese test basis is related to the IEC 60335 family but includes China-specific deviations, marking, plug, supply, and certification controls. India requires India-specific evidence and cannot rely on the existence of a CCC certificate alone.
china_standards: China CCC framework — domestic appliance safety certification route
GB 4706.1 and GB 4706.13 — Chinese appliance safety test bases
rows.gap: The gap is not only test clauses; it includes India plug and supply assumptions, marking language, importer identity, rated voltage/frequency, instruction manual content, critical component approvals, factory quality controls, and surveillance response. If a CB Scheme report exists, it may reduce engineering re-test scope, but the report must still be reviewed against IS 302 deviations and BIS acceptance practice.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where Indian law, BIS orders, licence conditions, registration conditions, or import obligations require electrical safety demonstration. IS 302 is a conformity benchmark / presumption tool, not a standalone legal mandate.
mandatory_status.cn: CCC safety evidence is China domestic evidence and does not automatically satisfy India IS 302 review or BIS acceptance.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Build an India electrical safety file around IS 302 review, India markings, importer details, and BIS acceptance practice. Chinese CCC/GB 4706 evidence can inform the technical review but should not be treated as a substitute for India-facing safety evidence.
